summary:
Lumbar drainage can effectively manage postoperative CSF fistulas after spine surgery by diverting CSF, especially when conservative measures fail. Its success depends on factors such as leak size—larger defects have lower response rates—but it remains a standard interim therapy prior to definitive surgical intervention.
